subject: Product Management
title: The Macintosh
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Macintosh.

hook.text: In 1984, Apple ran one Super Bowl ad and launched a computer that changed the relationship between humans and machines.

buildup.text: Before the Mac, computers required typing cryptic commands. Steve Jobs insisted on a graphical interface anyone could use.

discovery.text: The Mac borrowed ideas from Xerox PARC — windows, icons, and a mouse — and made them consumer-friendly.

twist.text: It was a commercial failure at first. Too expensive, too slow, too little software. The vision was ahead of the market.

climax.text: But the Mac established a product philosophy: technology should adapt to people, not the other way around.

punchline.text: The Mac didn't sell millions at first. It changed expectations forever.

quiz.question: What was the Mac's most important product contribution?
options: Establishing that technology should adapt to people, not the reverse
Inventing the graphical user interface from scratch
